name: Kindergarten Martial Arts — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice through a close look at focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice.

units.title: Wonder and notice: focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in focus, self-control, movement sequences, and calming practice.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
